info.title: each::labs Official API v1
info.description:
Official each::labs API V1.
This API provides access to our comprehensive AI model catalog and other resources.

## Public contract notes

- Public API routes handled by request logging include `X-Request-Id` for
  support and log correlation. Clients may send `X-Request-Id`; otherwise
  api-service generates one. Root, health, and metrics endpoints are excluded.
- REST error bodies keep the backward-compatible JSON shape used by existing
  clients. Most errors include at least `error`; some shared error paths also
  include `status` and `details`. Use the HTTP status code as the primary
  programmatic signal unless an endpoint documents a richer envelope.
- Rate-limit and retry headers are endpoint-dependent. If a response includes
  `Retry-After`, honor it before retrying; otherwise use bounded exponential
  backoff for retryable 429/5xx responses.
- api-service exposes single workflow trigger through
  `/v1/workflows/trigger/{workflowID}/{versionID}`. Bulk workflow trigger is
  not routed through api-service; use the workflows-engine public API directly
  where bulk trigger is required.

tags.name: AI Models
tags.description: Endpoints for listing and retrieving AI models and other resources.

tags.name: Uploads
tags.description: Endpoints for uploading files that can be used as model inputs.

tags.name: Workflows
tags.description: Endpoints for triggering workflow executions through api-service.

tags.name: OpenAI Compatibility
tags.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoints for LLM Router requests.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Endpoints for retrieving webhook information and delivery history.

**Note:** Currently, webhooks are only supported for Workflows V2. We are actively expanding webhook support to other services and will update this documentation as new integrations become available.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of AI models with optional filters

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by model name (case-insensitive search)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip for pagination

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific model by slug

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Model slug identifier

get.description: Retrieve the curated visible model list and generated model enum schema for eachlabs-llm-router.

200.description: LLM router model catalog retrieved successfully

post.description: Create a new prediction for a specific model.

input.prompt: A beautiful sunset over the ocean with vibrant colors

post.description:
Create a prediction and wait for the result. This compatibility endpoint is only supported
for models with synchronous execution enabled; unsupported models return a bad request error.

output.text: A cinematic sunset over the ocean.

get.description:
Retrieve the status and results of a model prediction by its ID.

Prediction statuses are `starting` or `processing` while work is in flight.
Terminal statuses are `success`, `failed`, and `cancelled`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Prediction ID

input.prompt: A beautiful sunset over the ocean with vibrant colors

post.description:
Cancel a running prediction by ID. Cancellation is best-effort for upstream providers;
not every provider can interrupt work that is already running. When provider
cancellation is supported and succeeds, the Eachlabs execution record is updated
to `cancelled`; otherwise the original prediction may still complete or fail.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Prediction ID

post.description:
Create a short-lived presigned URL for uploading media files. Upload the raw file bytes
to `presigned_url` using every header returned in `required_headers`, then pass
`public_url` as a model input.

delete.description:
Permanently delete a file you previously uploaded via the presign flow. Pass the `id`
returned by `POST /v1/upload/presign`. A successful delete removes the stored object, so the
file's `public_url` stops resolving. Deletes are idempotent — deleting a file that is already
gone also returns `204`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The file id returned by the presign step (the `id` field of the presign response).

204.description: File deleted, or already absent. Empty body.

get.description:
Retrieve execution history for the authenticated API key with optional filters.
Model prediction terminal statuses include `success`, `failed`, and `cancelled`;
workflow executions can surface `running`, `completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`
depending on the originating backend.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of executions to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of executions to skip for pagination

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter executions by model slug

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Comma-separated status filter

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: Filter by workflow ID

parameters.name: workflow_execution_id
parameters.description: Filter by workflow execution ID

parameters.name: error_classification
parameters.description: Comma-separated canonical error classification filter. Restricts results to terminal error executions whose classification is in the set; an unrecognized value returns 400. Each value must be one of the listed enum members.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of creation time window, as RFC 3339

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of creation time window, as RFC 3339

post.description:
OpenAI-compatible chat completions endpoint backed by Eachlabs LLM Router. Use
`Authorization: Bearer <API_KEY>` and point an OpenAI-compatible client at
`https://api.eachlabs.ai/v1`.

This endpoint uses the OpenAI-compatible error envelope rather than the REST
error body described in the global contract notes.

parameters.name: X-Eachlabs-Webhook-Url
parameters.description: Optional webhook URL for asynchronous result delivery.

200.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completion. Requests with `stream: true` return Server-Sent Events.

schema.description:
Server-Sent Events stream for requests with `stream: true`.
Events use OpenAI-compatible `chat.completion.chunk` payloads
and the stream terminates with `data: [DONE]`.

post.description:
Start an asynchronous workflow execution through api-service. This route forwards to
workflows-engine and requires callers to pin the workflow version in the path.

The immediate response only confirms the workflow was queued. Downstream workflow
lifecycle statuses are `running`, `completed`, `failed`, and `cancelled`.
api-service does not expose workflows-engine's bulk trigger route.

parameters.name: workflowID
parameters.description: Workflow ID or slug to execute.

parameters.name: versionID
parameters.description: Workflow version ID to execute.

parameters.name: webhook_url
parameters.description: Optional compatibility query parameter for execution completion webhooks. The request body field takes precedence when both are present.

inputs.prompt: A cinematic establishing shot of a mountain lake

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of webhooks for the authenticated organization

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of webhooks to return

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of webhooks to skip for pagination

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific webhook including delivery attempts by execution ID

parameters.name: execution_id
parameters.description: Execution ID for tracking the webhook

RequestId.description: Request correlation ID. Echoes the incoming `X-Request-Id` header or a generated ID.

RetryAfter.description: Optional retry delay returned by rate-limit or upstream retry responses when available.

BearerAuth.description: API key passed as an Authorization Bearer token for OpenAI-compatible endpoints

version.description: Deprecated. Always returns an empty string. Will be removed in a future version.

request_schema.description:
JSON Schema for API requests to this model.
Defines the structure, types, and validation rules for making requests.

version.description: Deprecated. Always returns an empty string. Will be removed in a future version.

request_schema.description:
JSON Schema for API requests to this model.
Defines the structure, types, and validation rules for making requests.
JSON Schema fragment containing the generated model enum.

inputs.description: Input values defined by the workflow version. May be omitted for workflows with no inputs.

example.prompt: A cinematic establishing shot of a mountain lake

webhook_url.description: Optional webhook URL for workflow execution completion.

webhook_secret.description: Optional secret used to sign workflow webhook requests.

status.description: Execution queue status returned immediately after triggering.

Error.description:
Backward-compatible REST error body. Endpoint-specific handlers may return only
`error`; shared error paths may also include `status` and `details`.

status.description: HTTP status code, when returned by the shared error handler.

details.description: Optional validation or request details for client errors.

version.description: Deprecated. This field is ignored. Kept for backwards compatibility.

example.prompt: A beautiful sunset over the ocean with vibrant colors

webhook_url.description: Optional webhook URL to receive prediction result asynchronously

webhook_secret.description: Optional secret used to sign webhook requests

status.description: Status of the prediction creation request

message.description: Human-readable message about the prediction creation

status.description: Final terminal status of the synchronous prediction

error.description: User-facing error message when the prediction failed

status.description: Cancellation status returned when the cancellation request succeeds

input.description: Input parameters used for the prediction

example.prompt: A beautiful sunset over the ocean with vibrant colors

status.description:
Current status of the prediction. `starting` and `processing` are
non-terminal. `success`, `failed`, and `cancelled` are terminal.

output.description: Prediction output (type varies based on model)

predict_time.description: Time taken for prediction in seconds

cost.description: Cost of the prediction in USD

content_type.description: MIME type of the file to upload

expires_in_seconds.description: How long the stored file is retained before automatic deletion, in seconds. Sets `expires_at` in the response. Defaults to 180 days when omitted; clamped to a minimum of 60 seconds and a maximum of 365 days (31536000). Does not affect the 15-minute upload-URL lifetime.

presigned_url.description: Short-lived URL for uploading raw file bytes

public_url.description: Public URL to pass as a model input after upload succeeds

expires_at.description: When the stored file is automatically deleted (its retention deadline). Defaults to 180 days out; controlled by `expires_in_seconds`. Not the upload-URL deadline — the presigned URL only accepts uploads for 15 minutes after issuance.

required_headers.description: Headers that must be sent verbatim with the upload PUT request

status.description:
Execution status. Model prediction terminal statuses include `success`,
`failed`, and `cancelled`; workflow executions can surface `running`,
`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled` depending on the originating backend.

workflow_id.description: Workflow ID, when execution was triggered by a workflow

workflow_execution_id.description: Workflow execution ID, when execution was triggered by a workflow

stream.description: Set to `true` to receive OpenAI-compatible SSE chunks (`text/event-stream`) instead of a buffered JSON response.

stream_options.description: Optional OpenAI-compatible streaming options. When `include_usage` is true, the stream may include a final usage chunk before `[DONE]`.

include_usage.description: Include token usage in the streamed response when provider usage data is available.

content.example: A cinematic sunset over the ocean.

OpenAIErrorResponse.description: OpenAI-compatible error envelope used only by `/v1/chat/completions`.

headers.description: Headers included in the webhook request

status_code.description: HTTP status code from target URL

example.error: Failed to fetch model: model not found

UnprocessableEntity.description: Request body could not be processed

example.error: Failed to fetch models: internal error
